<p>Swiss International said that a flight from Delhi to Zurich had to abort its takeoff early on Sunday after an engine issue, adding that four passengers and one crew member were injured.</p>

news.headline: Deadly Israeli strikes hit Lebanon despite ceasefire extension
news.summary: Lebanese state media reported a series of new Israeli strikes in at least four different locations in the country's south late Saturday, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered his military to attack Hezbollah following alleged ceasefire breaches. news.headline: Islamabad Reopens After U.S.-Iran Talks Fail to Materialize
news.summary: Officials had locked the city down, anticipating talks between U.S. and Iranian delegations. But they didn’t happen. “What did I close my business for?” one business owner asked.

news.headline: China unveils world’s first coal fuel cell that can produce electricity with zero emission
news.summary:
Chinese scientists have developed a way to generate electricity and achieve higher energy efficiency than conventional burning methods, while producing zero carbon dioxide emissions, by placing coal inside a “battery”.

news.headline: China Gen Z snipers nicknamed ‘Sweetheart’, ‘Gun Queen’ challenge gender stereotypes
news.summary:
Two female Gen Z snipers in China have drawn attention on the training ground, defying gender stereotypes with their marksmanship and grit.
Liu Jiangtao, 26, from Chongqing in southwestern China, serves as a sniper in a mobile unit of the Sichuan corps of the People’s Armed Police (PAP).

news.headline: What Happened After The New York Times Found a Cartel Mine on a Colombian Military Base
news.summary: Officers denied that an illegal, large-scale gold operation was underway within earshot of their posts. But we had seen it with our own eyes.

news.headline: Bus blast in Colombia kills 13 amid rising violence
news.summary: An explosive device killed 13 people traveling on a bus in southwestern Colombia on Saturday, an attack the country's army chief described as a “terrorist act" that also left at least 38 injured as violence linked to drug trafficking in the region escalates. FRANCE 24's Léa Hurel reports.

news.headline: Yemen’s landmine crisis endures despite truce and de-mining efforts
news.summary: Landmines in Yemen continue to kill and maim, even amid ceasefire efforts and ongoing de-mining initiatives.

news.headline: 2 US citizens killed in raid on Philippines communist stronghold
news.summary:
Two US citizens were among 19 people killed in a raid on an alleged communist stronghold in the Philippines that has sparked a probe by the country’s rights commission, a government task force said.
